text.title: Inbound Intake Qualification Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for inbound-intake-qualification. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every inbound lead MUST be scored against 5 questions: budget defined, decider identified, timeline within 6 months, scope describable in one sentence, fit with offering. Each scored 0 / 1 / 2.
rule.rationale: Sandler / Sandler-style BANT extension — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: Score 8-10 → discovery call within 48h. Score 5-7 → async clarifying questions. Score 0-4 → reject with templated decline.
rule.rationale: Lead-qualification practice — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: Every reject MUST go via a templated decline that offers (a) why fit is unclear, (b) a recommended alternative resource. Silent ghosting forbidden.
rule.rationale: Reputation-management practice — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: If decider is unknown, qualifying call collects the decider's name + access path; refusal to share decider is auto-reject.
rule.rationale: Sales-cycle research — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: If stated or implied budget is below your minimum, the lead is auto-rejected — you do not negotiate below the floor.
rule.rationale: Pricing-discipline practice — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: The rubric is reviewed quarterly against close-rate data; questions that don't predict conversion are dropped or replaced.
rule.rationale: Continuous improvement (Lean) — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: Every qualification decision is logged with the score + final outcome; the log feeds the quarterly tuning.
rule.rationale: Operational hygiene — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.

rule.statement: If inbound volume is below 1/month or all leads come from one referral channel, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Inbound Intake Qualification.
